DataProcessing.title: Data Processing Agreement
DataProcessing.subtitle: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
intro: This Data Processing Agreement (this Agreement) forms part of the Terms of Service (the Main Agreement) and is entered into by and between: (i) the customer (the Data Controller); and (ii) Compute Rental (the Data Processor or Service Provider). This Agreement takes effect on the date the customer and the processor enter into the Main Agreement.

Terms used in this Agreement have the meanings given in this Agreement. Capitalized terms not otherwise defined have the meanings assigned in the Main Agreement. Except as modified below, the Main Agreement remains in full force and effect.

definitions.title: Article 1. Definitions
definitions.intro: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the meanings set out below:

items.text: means information relating to a group of consumers and/or individuals where: (i) the Controller cannot be identified as the source of the information; (ii) information that would permit identification of an individual has been removed; and (iii) the information cannot reasonably identify or be linked to any consumer, individual, household, or device.
means the GDPR.
means any personal data processed by a Contracted Processor on behalf of the Controller under the Main Agreement.
means the Processor or a Subprocessor.
means the European Economic Area.
means Regulation (EU) 2016/679, the General Data Protection Regulation, and its implementing regulations in the European Economic Area and the United Kingdom.
means a transfer of Personal Data subject to the GDPR out of the European Economic Area.
means the services and other activities provided by the Contracted Processor to the Controller under the Main Agreement.
means the standard contractual clauses for transfers of personal data to third countries under European Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2021/914.

means any person appointed by the Processor or its affiliate to process Personal Data on behalf of any Controller, including third parties and Processor affiliates, but excluding employees of the Processor.
means an entity that owns, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with the Processor.

definitions.gdprTerms: The terms Commission, Controller, Data Subject, Member State, Personal Data, Personal Data Breach, Processing, and Supervisory Authority have the same meanings as in the GDPR.

processorObligations.title: 2.1 Processor obligations
processorObligations.intro: The Processor and Processor Affiliates shall:
items: comply with all Applicable Law when processing Personal Data;

not process Personal Data except on the Controller's written instructions unless required by Applicable Law. If Applicable Law requires such processing, the Processor or relevant Processor Affiliate shall notify the Controller of that legal requirement before processing the relevant Personal Data, unless the law prohibits such notice.

controllerObligations.title: 2.2 Controller obligations
items: instruct the Processor and each Processor Affiliate, and authorize them to instruct each Subprocessor, to process Personal Data, including transfers to any country or territory, to the extent reasonably necessary to provide the Services and consistent with the Main Agreement.

confidentiality.title: 2.3 Confidentiality
confidentiality.text: The Processor confirms that all Personal Data received from the Controller, the Controller's employees, or consultants, or obtained in connection with performance of the Services under the Main Agreement, shall be treated by the Processor as confidential information and protected accordingly.

usageLimits.title: 2.4 Use restrictions
usageLimits.text: The Processor shall not directly or indirectly sell any Personal Data, use any Personal Data for purposes other than providing the Services to the Controller, or retain, use, or disclose any Personal Data outside the scope of this Agreement or the Main Agreement.

anonymizedData.title: 2.5 Anonymized Data
anonymizedData.text: The Processor may use Anonymized Data for its own purposes.

controllerRepresentations.title: 2.6 Controller representations and warranties
controllerRepresentations.intro: The Controller represents and warrants that:
items: it is, at all relevant times, duly and validly authorized to issue the instructions described in this article;
it has all rights necessary to provide Personal Data to the Processor for processing related to the Services;
one or more lawful bases under Applicable Law support the lawfulness of the processing;
it has provided all necessary privacy notices to Data Subjects;
it has obtained all necessary Data Subject consents for processing and retains records of those consents;
if a Data Subject withdraws consent and no other lawful basis remains for retaining that Data Subject's Personal Data, the Controller will communicate that withdrawal to the Processor.

personnel.title: Article 3. Processor and Affiliate Personnel
personnel.text: The Processor and each Processor Affiliate shall take reasonable steps to ensure the reliability of any employee, agent, or contractor of a Contracted Processor who may have access to Personal Data. In each case, access shall be limited to personnel who strictly need to know or access the relevant Personal Data for purposes of the Main Agreement, and all such personnel shall be bound by confidentiality commitments or professional or statutory confidentiality obligations.

security.title: Article 4. Security
items: Taking into account the state of the art, implementation costs, and the nature, scope, context, and purposes of processing, as well as the risks of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Processor and Processor Affiliates shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures for Personal Data to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk.

When assessing the appropriate level of security, the Processor and each Processor Affiliate shall consider in particular the risks presented by processing, especially the risk of Personal Data Breach.

The parties acknowledge that security requirements change over time and effective security requires frequent evaluation and regular improvement of outdated measures. The parties shall negotiate in good faith regarding the costs, if any, of implementing material changes required by Applicable Law or a competent Supervisory Authority.

If the Main Agreement must be amended to carry out the Controller's instructions to improve security measures, including as Applicable Law may require from time to time, the parties shall negotiate such amendment in good faith.

restrictedTransfers.title: Article 5. Restricted Transfers
items: The Processor shall not export, transfer, or store Personal Data in, or remotely access Personal Data from, any country or territory outside the European Economic Area that has not received an adequacy decision from the European Commission, unless appropriate safeguards are provided. Such safeguards may include: (i) standard data protection clauses under GDPR Article 46.2(c) or (d); (ii) binding corporate rules under GDPR Article 46.2(b); (iii) derogations for specific situations under Article 49; or (iv) any other GDPR-recognized instrument approved by the European Commission or a Supervisory Authority.

To the extent no adequacy decision or other appropriate safeguard applies, the parties agree that the Standard Contractual Clauses are incorporated into this Agreement. The Controller shall be the data exporter and the Processor shall be the data importer.

subprocessing.title: Article 6. Subprocessing
items: The Controller authorizes the Processor and each Processor Affiliate to appoint Subprocessors subject to this article and the restrictions in the Main Agreement.
The Processor and each Processor Affiliate may continue using Subprocessors already engaged as of the date this Agreement is entered into.
The Processor shall provide the Controller, upon reasonable request, a list of any new Subprocessors engaged after this Agreement is entered into.
subprocessing.responsibilitiesIntro: For each Subprocessor, the Processor or Processor Affiliate shall:
responsibilities: ensure its arrangement with the Subprocessor is governed by a written contract whose terms provide at least the same level of Personal Data protection as this Agreement;

if the arrangement involves a Restricted Transfer, ensure the Standard Contractual Clauses or another approved transfer mechanism under the GDPR is incorporated into the agreement between the parties at the relevant time.

dataSubjectRights.title: Article 7. Data Subject Rights
dataSubjectRights.assistance: Taking into account the nature of the processing, the Processor and each Processor Affiliate shall assist each Controller, through appropriate technical and organizational measures to the extent feasible, in fulfilling its obligation to respond to Data Subject requests to exercise rights under Applicable Law.
processorItems: notify the Controller when the Processor or a Processor Affiliate receives a request from a Data Subject under any Applicable Law regarding Personal Data;

ensure that the Contracted Processor does not respond to the request unless required by Applicable Law, in which case the Processor shall notify the Controller of the legal requirement before the Contracted Processor responds, unless the law prohibits such notice.
dataSubjectRights.controller: The Controller shall be responsible for responding to Data Subject requests concerning Personal Data as required by any Applicable Law.

controllerAssistance.title: Article 8. Assistance to the Data Controller
controllerAssistance.text: Taking into account the nature of the processing and the information available to the Processor, the Processor shall, at the Controller's expense, assist the Controller with data protection impact assessments and prior consultation with Supervisory Authorities. The Controller and Processor shall negotiate reasonable fees in good faith before such assistance begins.

personalDataBreach.title: Article 9. Personal Data Breach
personalDataBreach.text: The Processor shall notify the Controller without undue delay once the Processor or a Subprocessor becomes aware of a Personal Data Breach affecting Personal Data.

audit.title: Article 10. Audit
audit.text: Upon the Controller's reasonable request, the Processor shall demonstrate the technical and organizational measures it has adopted under this Agreement and permit the Controller to audit and test those measures.
items: A Controller conducting an audit shall give the Processor or relevant Processor Affiliate reasonable advance notice and shall use reasonable efforts to avoid causing damage, injury, or disruption when its personnel enter a Contracted Processor's premises for an audit or inspection.

The Contracted Processor is not required to provide premises access for an audit or inspection where any individual fails to present reasonable proof of identity and authorization, outside normal business hours of the premises except for an urgent audit, or more than once in any calendar year except for additional audits required by Applicable Law or a Supervisory Authority.

Unless Applicable Law or a Supervisory Authority requires otherwise, the Contracted Processor may satisfy the audit requirement in this article by providing its latest SOC 2 audit report or an equivalent report to the Controller under a confidentiality agreement.

returnDeletion.title: Article 11. Deletion or Return of Personal Data
items: Within 30 days after the termination date, the Controller may request by written notice that the Processor and each Processor Affiliate either (a) return to the Controller a complete copy of all Personal Data and/or (b) delete and procure deletion of all other copies of Personal Data processed by the Contracted Processors. The Processor and each Processor Affiliate shall comply with such written request within 90 days after receipt.
Each Contracted Processor may retain Personal Data to the extent required by Applicable Law and shall ensure that such Personal Data is processed only for the purposes required by Applicable Law.

governingLaw.title: Article 12. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
items: The parties to this Agreement submit to the choice of jurisdiction set out in the Main Agreement;

This Agreement and all non-contractual or other obligations arising out of or in connection with it are governed by the laws of the country or territory specified for that purpose in the Main Agreement.

appendix.title: Appendix A: Standard Contractual Clauses
paragraphs: The Standard Contractual Clauses (controller-to-processor) are incorporated into this Agreement in accordance with European Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2021/914 and provide appropriate safeguards for privacy and fundamental rights in cross-border transfers of Personal Data.

The clauses cover core matters including data protection obligations, Data Subject rights, remedies, liability, supervision, local laws and practices affecting compliance, notification obligations, non-compliance and termination, governing law, and dispute resolution.

annexOne.title: Annex I: Data Processing Information
dataCategories: General contact information, including but not limited to telephone numbers and email addresses
processingOperations: Processing carried out to provide the services described in the Main Agreement
Data will be transferred on a continuing basis
Personal Data will be retained for as long as necessary to maintain the business relationship and provide the services

annexTwo.title: Annex II: Technical and Organizational Measures
annexTwo.text: The data importer will implement and maintain security standards at least equivalent to the level of protection specified in the data processing and security terms. Measures include:

measures.text: measures to prevent unauthorized access to IT systems, including password policies covering special characters, minimum length, forced password changes, and prohibitions on guest users or anonymous accounts
measures to prevent authorized users from accessing data beyond their authorization scope
encryption at rest (AES 256) and encryption in transit across the cloud infrastructure

annexTwo.customerSecurity: The customer is responsible for securing its user credentials and for adopting appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the networks, endpoints, and browsers used to access the services.

annexThree.title: Annex III: Subprocessors
annexThree.text: The Controller has authorized use of the following Subprocessors:
items: Stripe (payment processing)
Google (analytics and infrastructure)
Meta (advertising and analytics)
Microsoft (infrastructure services)

metadata.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy | Compute Rental
metadata.description: Security vulnerability disclosure policy and reporting guidance for the Compute Rental platform.

purpose.title: Policy purpose
purpose.text: Compute Rental welcomes responsible security research and coordinated disclosure. This policy explains how to report potential vulnerabilities, what researchers can expect from our handling process, and the legal protection we provide without committing to monetary rewards.

bugBounty.title: Bug bounty program
bugBounty.text: Although we do not guarantee monetary rewards for submitted reports, Compute Rental may, at its sole discretion, provide a bug bounty for high-value reports. All rewards are discretionary and are not guaranteed. Participation in this program should not be driven solely by compensation.

scope.title: Scope
inScopeItems: Compute Rental web console, REST APIs, and billing flows
Default Docker and KVM isolation based on reference images
GPU memory isolation and tenant escape vulnerabilities
outOfScopeItems: User workloads and third-party container images
Social engineering, physical security, or denial-of-service (DoS) testing
Brute-force attacks against customer passwords or multi-factor authentication
Any activity that violates applicable law, export regulations, or supplier terms of service
scope.note: If you are unsure whether a target is in scope, contact us first through the channel listed in the How to report section.

safeHarbor.title: Safe harbor
items: Research conducted under this policy is considered authorized. Compute Rental will not pursue civil or criminal action for good-faith, accidental violations.
We use industry gold-standard safe harbor terms to protect good-faith security researchers.
Safe harbor does not apply to activity on third-party infrastructure that we do not control, such as upstream data centers.

items.text: Avoid privacy violations, service interruption, or data destruction.
Please use your own account for testing.

items.term: Stop and report immediately if you encounter user data.
items.text: If you encounter user data, including personal information, health information, payment information, model checkpoints, or similar data, stop immediately and report it.
Give Compute Rental 90 days to remediate before public disclosure unless we agree to an earlier date.

commitment.title: Our commitment
commitment.text: We will keep you informed of handling progress and provide safe harbor protection to good-faith security researchers.

reporting.title: How to report
reporting.intro: Please include the following information in your report:
items: Vulnerability summary
Affected service
Step-by-step reproduction instructions
Impact assessment
Any proof-of-concept code or screenshots

quality.title: Report quality requirements
quality.text: Reports with clear descriptions, precise reproduction steps, and concise tool output can be triaged faster. We strongly encourage including proof-of-concept code.

notAccepted.title: Findings we do not accept
notAccepted.text: Compute Rental is not interested in theoretical or extremely low-probability vulnerabilities, or findings without a demonstrable security impact. Examples include:
items: Clickjacking without security impact
SPF or DMARC configuration issues on domains not owned by Compute Rental
Outdated TLS cipher suites on assets that do not involve sensitive data
Best-practice recommendations without an exploitable vulnerability
Issues affecting only browsers or operating systems that are no longer supported
Dangling-link takeover, tabnabbing, content spoofing, or text injection without further impact
Attacks requiring physical access
Self-XSS or self-DoS that only affects your own account
CSRF on non-critical forms, such as logout
Permissive CORS without an exploitation path
CSV injection or open redirects not linked to a real attack

legal.title: Legal statement
legal.intro: By participating in this program, you acknowledge that:
items: You have read and will comply with this policy.
Compute Rental may use any submitted information to remediate vulnerabilities.
Compute Rental reserves all rights to modify or terminate this policy at any time. Changes will be published at least 30 days before they take effect.

contact.title: Questions?
contact.subject: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Inquiry
contact.suffix: , and we will respond within two business days.
contact.thanks: Thank you for helping protect the Compute Rental platform and community.

metadata.title: Compliance | Compute Rental
metadata.description: Security compliance certifications and data protection standards for the Compute Rental platform.

intro.description: Compute Rental applies rigorous security controls and maintains multiple compliance certifications to protect customer data. As a GPU compute platform serving AI startups, research institutions, and enterprises, we hold ourselves to the same standards our customers require.

items.title: SOC 2 Type 3
items.text: Our SOC 2 Type 3 report is available promptly upon request. To obtain it, please contact the sales team.

items.title: SOC 2 Type 2
items.text: Compute Rental has completed SOC 2 Type 2 certification. The audit was performed by an independent third party and validates that our security, availability, and confidentiality controls met AICPA Trust Services Criteria over the observation period. The report is available after execution of a nondisclosure agreement; contact the sales team to request access.

items.title: HIPAA
items.text: Compute Rental supports HIPAA-aligned workloads at the secure cloud tier. Technical safeguards include data isolation, access controls, and audit logging designed to meet HIPAA requirements. Eligible customers may execute a Business Associate Agreement (BAA).

items.title: GDPR
items.text: We comply with the General Data Protection Regulation for all European users. Our Data Processing Agreement describes processing practices, subprocessor disclosures, and Data Subject rights. EU-region compute resources can be provided upon request.

items.title: Data privacy compliance
items.text: Compute Rental complies with applicable data privacy laws and regulations. Our Privacy Policy describes data collection, use, retention, and deletion practices.

sections.title: Customer data isolation
items: Each workload runs in a separate, unprivileged Docker container fully isolated from other tenants
Customers can access only their own data; there is no shared file system between tenants
Data is destroyed immediately after a customer deletes an instance

sections.title: Network and access control
items: All API and console traffic is encrypted in transit with TLS 1.2+
Role-based access control governs internal systems
All programmatic access uses API key authentication

sections.title: Monitoring and incident response
items: Continuous monitoring for anomalous activity across the platform
Documented incident response processes with clear escalation paths
Regular internal and third-party security audits

sections.title: Employee security
items: All employees undergo background checks
Security and compliance training is provided at onboarding and annually thereafter
All internal access follows the principle of least privilege

securityTiers.title: Security tiers
securityTiers.description: Compute Rental offers two security tiers to support different requirements:

certifiedHost.title: Certified hosts
certifiedHost.description: Suitable for general AI and high-performance computing workloads:
items: Manually tested for reliability and performance
Docker-level tenant isolation
Cost-efficient option for non-regulated workloads

secureCloud.title: Secure Cloud (trusted data centers)
secureCloud.description: Designed for regulated industries and enterprise security requirements:

sections.title: Data center partner requirements:
items: Equipment is hosted in professionally managed data center facilities
At least five GPU servers with flagship-grade hardware
Data Processing Agreement executed with Compute Rental
Due diligence on facility security, ownership, and business identity

sections.title: Partner certifications may include:
items: SOC 1 Type 2, SOC 2 Type 2, SOC 3
HIPAA, HITRUST, PCI DSS
NIST framework and GDPR compliance

sections.title: Physical and environmental security:
items: Facility access uses biometric or keycard authentication
Video surveillance is retained for more than 90 days
Fire detection and suppression systems
Redundant power and temperature-control systems
Annual testing of all environmental control systems

sections.title: Audit and oversight:
items: Compute Rental audits data center partners for equity structure, identity, and source of funds
Ongoing verification that partners maintain facility standards and follow best practices

legalProtections.title: Legal and contractual safeguards
items: The Data Processing Agreement governs all data processing activities
The Privacy Policy describes data collection, use, and retention practices
The Terms of Service define platform obligations and customer rights
Secure Cloud data center hosts execute extended hosting agreements with additional Data Processing Agreement terms

securityRecord.title: Security record
securityRecord.text: Compute Rental has maintained a clean security record since launch, with no major security incidents.

metadata.title: Use Cases | Compute Rental
metadata.description: Run AI training, inference, rendering, transcription, and other workloads quickly and flexibly on high-performance GPUs.

hero.description: From training and fine-tuning to rendering and transcription, run demanding workloads on high-performance GPUs with flexible on-demand capacity.

items.title: AI text generation
items.desc: Deploy large language models for text generation, conversational AI, content creation, and code assistance with low-latency GPU inference.

items.title: AI image and video generation
items.desc: Run Stable Diffusion, Midjourney alternatives, and video-generation models to turn prompts into production-ready creative output.

items.title: AI agents
items.desc: Build and run autonomous AI agents for task automation, decision support, and complex workflow orchestration.

items.title: Batch data processing
items.desc: Accelerate large data pipelines, ETL jobs, and batch inference workloads across terabyte-scale datasets.

items.title: Audio-to-text transcription
items.desc: Use models such as Whisper for accurate speech recognition and transcription across multilingual, streaming, and batch workloads.

items.title: AI fine-tuning
items.desc: Fine-tune foundation models on private datasets with LoRA, QLoRA, and other efficient methods to create task-specific models.

items.title: Virtual computing
items.desc: Launch high-performance virtual desktops and remote workstations for AI development, data science, and professional workflows.

items.title: GPU programming
items.desc: Access CUDA, OpenCL, and GPU compute resources directly for scientific computing, simulation, and custom kernel development.

items.title: Graphics rendering
items.desc: Use GPU acceleration for 3D rendering, animation, and visual effects with engines such as Blender and Unreal Engine.

highlights.title: Open-model workflows, full control, no platform premium
highlights.subtitle: Access high-performance GPUs directly, run open-source models with prebuilt templates, and keep your stack under your control.

items.title: Ready-to-use templates
items.desc: Skip environment setup and launch fully configured GPU instances in minutes with prebuilt templates.

items.title: Low-cost GPU capacity
items.desc: Use a 20,000+ GPU resource pool with competitive pricing across RTX 5090, 4090, H100, and H200 instances.

items.title: Open source first
items.desc: Run vLLM, TGI, ComfyUI, and other open frameworks without proprietary licensing fees or platform markups.

items.title: Flexible deployment
items.desc: Bring your own models, scale inference your way, and resize capacity on demand without wasted spend.

cta.titleLine2: running AI workloads on our platform

ai-text-generation.description: Launch and scale modern large language models in minutes with GPU-accelerated distributed inference.
builtFor: Start open-source models such as LLaMA 3 and DeepSeek, or load your own fine-tuned checkpoints.

Serve models through a clean API or WebUI with less manual setup before launch.
Keep full control of the runtime. Models run on isolated GPUs and data can be cleared to your requirements.

ai-image-video-generation.title: AI image and video generation
ai-image-video-generation.description: Generate high-quality images and video efficiently with GPU-powered creative workflows.
ai-image-video-generation.imageAlt: AI image and video generation workflow
builtFor: Generate high-resolution output with models such as Stable Diffusion and Disco Diffusion.
Start preconfigured creative environments optimized for generation workloads in minutes.
Access powerful GPUs for compute-heavy rendering while keeping budgets controlled.
Use Stable Diffusion and Disco Diffusion templates to simplify deployment and resource management.

ai-agents.title: AI agents
ai-agents.description: Deploy and scale AI agent workloads on cost-effective GPU compute.
builtFor: Run the frameworks you already use, including LangChain, Langflow, AutoGen, CrewAI, or custom code.
Scale agent workloads smoothly from a single node to distributed clusters.
Use usage dashboards to track GPU, CPU, and cost metrics while paying by actual runtime.
Package Docker images, libraries, CUDA, and driver versions into reusable templates.
Switch to open-source equivalents through OpenAI-compatible chat endpoints and reduce per-token cost.

builtFor: Automate large data transformation, cleaning, and preparation tasks.
Allocate compute resources dynamically around batch job requirements.
Reduce idle cost with usage-based billing and interruptible instance options.
Connect easily to existing data pipelines or cloud storage.

audio-to-text-transcription.title: Audio-to-text transcription
audio-to-text-transcription.description: Convert audio into accurate text quickly with GPU-driven transcription workloads.
builtFor: Use open-source models to turn audio files into accurate transcripts.
Process large transcription workloads through scalable GPU access.
Support multilingual and common audio formats inside locked containers.
Start ready-to-use speech-to-text environments through one-click actions or CLI.

ai-fine-tuning.title: AI fine-tuning
ai-fine-tuning.description: Improve model behavior with efficient, on-demand fine-tuning workflows.
builtFor: Train and optimize pretrained models on your own datasets for task-specific results.
Use powerful GPUs to shorten training time and reduce cost.
Match storage, memory, and compute resources to model scale.
Deploy fine-tuned models into inference environments after training.

virtual-computing.title: Virtual computing
virtual-computing.description: Provision GPU-backed virtual machines with flexible, secure access.
builtFor: Launch GPU-backed virtual desktops or remote workstations in seconds.
Run Linux or Ubuntu with full access to your preferred tools and workflows.
Resize resources on demand without changing cloud providers or plans.
Keep work isolated inside dedicated environments.

gpu-programming.title: GPU programming
gpu-programming.description: Accelerate AI and HPC breakthroughs with scalable GPU compute performance.
builtFor: Access raw GPU compute for custom CUDA application development.
Optimize performance for specific architectures such as A100 and 4090.
Use full admin access to configure drivers, memory, and execution environments.
Test and iterate across GPU types with minimal setup.

3d-rendering.title: Graphics rendering
3d-rendering.description: Accelerate 3D rendering and graphics workflows with powerful GPU acceleration.
builtFor: Render detail-rich 3D models faster with GPU acceleration.
Reduce frame time and scene-processing time for complex animation or visualization.
Access professional-grade GPUs without long-term contracts.
Support VFX, product design, architecture, and other rendering workflows.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Key details about compute rental, product resources, and billing.

items.question: What types of GPUs can I rent?
items.answer: We offer a range of high-performance GPU resources, including NVIDIA H100, A100, RTX 4090, and similar options for AI training, inference, and compute tasks at different scales.

items.question: How does billing work?
items.answer: The platform uses transparent usage-based billing, typically charged by actual runtime with no hidden fees. Long-term or steady workloads can also use more suitable pricing plans.

items.question: Is there a minimum rental duration?
items.answer: There is no strict minimum rental limit. You can start instances on demand for short experiments or keep them running for production workloads.

items.question: What technical support is available?
items.answer: We provide support for compute infrastructure and help with deployment, connectivity, runtime issues, resource usage, and practical optimization.

items.question: Can compute resources scale dynamically?
items.answer: Yes. You can choose and adjust GPU nodes from the console as workloads change, matching resource scale to training, inference, or batch-processing needs.

comparison.title: When standard storage is not enough
paragraphs: For most AI workloads, AI Object Storage and Distributed File Storage are the recommended starting point. They are built for GPU-accelerated pipelines, zero-egress pricing, and Kubernetes integration.

Hybrid environments, strict compliance needs, and deep VAST ecosystem dependencies are different. Those workloads need the isolation, control, and extensibility that only a dedicated cluster can provide.

items.question: What is Dedicated VAST Storage?
items.answer: Dedicated VAST Storage is a single-tenant VAST AI Operating System deployment on dedicated hardware, giving workloads full platform access.

items.question: When should I choose Dedicated VAST Storage?
items.answer: It is best suited for workloads that need strict data isolation, advanced security controls, hybrid replication, or deep VAST ecosystem capabilities.

items.question: How does it integrate with GPU workloads?
items.answer: Dedicated VAST clusters can be deployed in the same data center as GPU infrastructure and attached through the standard VAST CSI driver to reduce data access latency.

cards.description: Metrics, logs, telemetry, and system signals.

items.title: Security and audit transparency
items.description: Mission Control provides real-time visibility into cluster access and activity. Telemetry Relay forwards encrypted audit and security events to your SIEM to support governance, compliance reviews, and operational trust.
items.mediaAlt: Mission Control security and audit transparency

items.title: Fleet lifecycle controller
items.description: Every node is continuously evaluated against the performance requirements of modern AI workloads. Fleet Lifecycle Controller tracks long-term GPU and node health, detects subtle degradation patterns, and replaces unhealthy nodes before they affect accuracy or throughput.

items.title: Node lifecycle controller
items.description: Mission Control continuously monitors node health regressions and automatically replaces nodes when thresholds are reached. Node Lifecycle Controller manages health from first deployment through the full node lifecycle, reducing interruptions, wasted GPU hours, and training drift.

items.title: Direct access to experts
items.description: When customers need deeper help, expert support routes requests to the engineers who build and operate the platform, helping issues get resolved quickly and accurately.

items.title: Observability and performance visibility
items.description: Mission Control unifies GPU metrics, networking, storage, orchestration, and workload behavior into a consistent signal layer. Teams can measure performance, diagnose issues, and recover jobs faster through familiar dashboards.

items.title: Audit and transparency
items.description: The observability layer and Telemetry Relay provide real-time visibility into access, activity, and system behavior. Audit and access logs can flow directly into SIEM or monitoring tools for governance, compliance reviews, and fast operational diagnosis.

items.title: GPU Straggler Detection preview
items.description: Distributed training does not fail gracefully. When one GPU falls behind, the whole job slows down. GPU Straggler Detection uses NVIDIA collective operation signals to identify the exact rank, GPU, and node causing slowdowns so root cause analysis is faster and more precise.

visibility.title: See everything. Understand faster. Act now.
visibility.description: Mission Control unifies the signals that matter most, including performance metrics, audit activity, workload behavior, and GPU-level anomalies, then turns them into immediate, actionable insight.

items.question: How does Mission Control improve reliability?
items.answer: Mission Control automates node and cluster health management through lifecycle controllers, CloudOps monitoring, and direct access to expert support.

items.question: Does Telemetry Relay only support audit logs?
items.answer: No. Telemetry Relay can forward audit and access logs, and can forward additional telemetry types to customer endpoints when enabled.

items.question: Can GPU Straggler Detection be used for inference jobs?
items.answer: GPU Straggler Detection is optimized for distributed training. Inference visibility is covered by the broader Mission Control observability metrics.

items.question: Does Mission Control include observability tools?
items.answer: Yes. Mission Control includes CoreWeave Observe for cluster-level metrics and dashboards, plus Telemetry Relay for audit and access visibility.

items.question: What is the CoreWeave Mission Control Agent?
items.answer: Mission Control Agent helps teams interpret system behavior in real time and can answer questions about GPU performance, slow training, and cluster health.

items.question: Is Mission Control billed separately?
items.answer: Mission Control is delivered as part of CoreWeave Cloud. Telemetry Relay can forward audit and access logs without adding separate log forwarding costs.

items.question: How does it integrate with existing observability and security tools?
items.answer: Mission Control works with existing SIEM, logging, and monitoring systems through Telemetry Relay and CoreWeave Observe.

code.codeString:
# Install SUNK with Helm
helm repo add coreweave https://helm.coreweave.com
helm repo update

helm install sunk coreweave/sunk \
  --namespace slurm \
  --create-namespace \
  -f sunk-values.yaml

# Verify controller status
kubectl get pods -n slurm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=sunk

why.title: Flexibility should not mean fragmentation
paragraphs: As training environments expand across providers and customer-owned infrastructure, teams risk accumulating different workflows, control planes, and operating practices. That fragmentation adds complexity where continuity matters most.
SUNK Anywhere extends the same SUNK system beyond CoreWeave, giving organizations a faster path to production training and less setup work as infrastructure choices grow.

hero.title: Diversity, inclusion, and belonging: unlocking our full potential

intro.title: Unlimited opportunity for everyone
intro.text: Solving the world’s biggest challenges requires new ideas shaped by different perspectives, backgrounds, and cultures. That diversity drives innovation and creativity, creating opportunity for everyone committed to turning ideas into reality. Everyone has a voice, and everyone belongs.

items.title: Connecting with great talent
items.description: We want to attract outstanding global talent that reflects a wide range of perspectives. We improve recruiting processes, create more inclusive job descriptions, and help candidates meet employees from communities they identify with during the interview experience.
items.quote: “I felt welcomed from the start and received the support and encouragement I needed to succeed. Wherever you come from, you are part of the team.”

items.title: Exploring allyship at work
items.description: We work to create an environment where underrepresented groups feel empowered. Through the CARE Allyship program, employees learn how to recognize the power of diversity and inclusion and take action to help others succeed.
items.quote: “The allyship program taught me to pay closer attention to the needs of underrepresented groups and to create space for people to be seen and heard.”

items.title: Navigating everyday work
items.description: We encourage and support employees who are ready to take on new challenges. Tailored mentoring, job-shadowing experiences, and leadership development opportunities help people explore what is possible.
items.quote: “The company created the conditions for me to thrive and strengthened the employee experience through inclusion and belonging.”

featured.quote: For the first time in my career, I can be myself without pretending to be someone else to succeed. As a proud Black refugee, this has been the happiest chapter of my career. Bringing my whole self to work has always been a dream.

support.title: Supporting what matters

items.title: Listening to employee needs
items.text: Employees matter most. We adapt to different needs and support people at every stage of their personal and professional growth.

items.title: Committed to excellence
items.text: We believe that when employees and their families are supported, people can bring their best selves to work every day.

support.quote: When I arrived, I was activated for deployment. The support provided to me and my family was game-changing, and I will never forget it.

items.title: Connect with others
items.text: Community resource groups play an important role in the employee experience, from leading celebrations to advocacy and culture building. We support groups focused on identity, neurodiversity, caregivers, working parents, and more.
list: Asian and Pacific Islander and Allies
items.quote: “As a co-lead for ADHD employees, I have experienced what it means for neurodiverse people to be seen and included. Neurodiversity helps me see how different parts connect to create world-class experiences.”

items.title: Build a more inclusive workplace
items.text: We partner with diverse student organizations across campuses, including minority-serving institutions, historically Black colleges and universities, and Hispanic-serving institutions. We also work with organizations such as AnitaB.org, Latinas in Tech, Women in Machine Learning, NSBE, and SHPE.

commitment.title: Our commitment to progress
